phase.name: Add tags prop and rendering to card components

task.name: Add tags display to ExpenseCard
task.action: In src/components/shared/ExpenseCard.tsx: 1. Add `tags?: { id: string; name: string; color: string }[]` to the ExpenseCardProps interface. 2. Add `tags` to the destructured props in the component function signature. 3. Badge is already imported -- no new import needed. 4. Insert the ADAPTED tag badges JSX block (see spec "Badge Markup" section -- uses text-xs, h-5, px-2, w-2 h-2 dot, gap-1.5) as a new full-width row AFTER the expenseType/expenseSubcategory paragraph (line 98) and BEFORE the date/amount footer div (line 100). The parent flex-col gap-3 provides spacing -- no mt class needed on the tag container. IMPORTANT: Do NOT use CompactTransactionCard's text-[10px]/h-4/px-1.5 sizing. Use the scaled-up values from the spec.
task.verify: npm run lint and npm run build pass with zero errors.

task.name: Add tags display to IncomeCard
task.action: In src/components/shared/IncomeCard.tsx: 1. Add `import { Badge } from '../ui/badge'` to the imports. 2. Add `tags?: { id: string; name: string; color: string }[]` to the IncomeCardProps interface. 3. Add `tags` to the destructured props. 4. Insert the ADAPTED tag badges JSX block (text-xs, h-5, px-2, w-2 h-2 dot, gap-1.5) as a new row AFTER the incomeType paragraph (line 53) and BEFORE the date/amount footer div (line 55). Parent gap-3 handles spacing.
task.verify: npm run lint and npm run build pass with zero errors.

task.name: Add tags display to TransferCard
task.action: In src/components/shared/TransferCard.tsx: 1. Add `import { Badge } from '../ui/badge'` to the imports. 2. Add `tags?: { id: string; name: string; color: string }[]` to the TransferCardProps type. 3. Add `tags` to the destructured props. 4. Insert the ADAPTED tag badges JSX block INSIDE the existing flex-col gap-1 metadata wrapper, AFTER the date paragraph (line 55) but BEFORE the closing div of that wrapper (line 56). Add mt-1 to the tag container div: className="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5 mt-1". This keeps tags grouped with the metadata, not as a separate card-level row. NOTE: TransferCard placement differs from ExpenseCard/IncomeCard because of its unique metadata wrapper structure.
task.verify: npm run lint and npm run build pass with zero errors.

phase.name: Pass tags prop from parent pages

task.name: Pass tags to ExpenseCard in expenses page
task.action: In src/app/expenses/page.tsx, inside the expenseTransactions.map() block (~line 217-239), add `tags={expense.tags}` as a prop on the ExpenseCard component.
task.verify: npm run lint and npm run build pass with zero errors.

task.name: Pass tags to IncomeCard in income page
task.action: In src/app/income/page.tsx, inside the incomeTransactions.map() block (~line 315-325), add `tags={income.tags}` as a prop on the IncomeCard component.
task.verify: npm run lint and npm run build pass with zero errors.

task.name: Pass tags to TransferCard in transfers page
task.action: In src/app/transfers/page.tsx, inside the transfers.map() block (~line 149-159), add `tags={transfer.tags}` as a prop on the TransferCard component.
task.verify: npm run lint and npm run build pass with zero errors.

task.name: Add tag rendering tests to ExpenseCard.test.tsx
task.action: In src/components/shared/ExpenseCard.test.tsx, add test cases: 1. "renders tag badges when tags are provided" -- pass 2 tags with name and color, assert both tag names appear in the document. 2. "shows overflow indicator when more than 3 tags" -- pass 5 tags, assert "+2 more" text appears and only 3 tag names render. 3. "renders no tags section when tags is undefined" -- pass no tags prop, assert no badge elements for tags appear.

task.name: Add tag rendering tests to IncomeCard.test.tsx
task.action: In src/components/shared/IncomeCard.test.tsx, add the same three test cases as ExpenseCard (tags rendering, overflow, no tags).

task.name: Add TransferCard.test.tsx with tag tests
task.action: Create src/components/shared/TransferCard.test.tsx if it does not exist. Include: 1. Basic rendering test (card displays name, formatted amount, from/to accounts, date, transfer type). 2. Tag badges rendering test (same pattern as ExpenseCard/IncomeCard tests). 3. Overflow indicator test. 4. No tags test. Mock ../icons (Icons.addTransfer), ../ui/badge (Badge), and lucide-react (ArrowRight) as needed.
